page: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The hardware configuration allocates 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical core, supporting 2 threads per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU resources, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2700.79. However, multi-core stress-ng performance is weak, and Geekbench multi-core scores are poor, placing in the bottom 10% at 1960.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks are also constrained, with small-block read-only bandwidth rated as poor at 127.98 GB/sec and large-block write bandwidth weak at 21.13 GB/sec. Passmark memory performance is average, scoring 2334.27.

This server type offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require heavy parallel processing. However, the low core count and weak multi-core scaling present a clear tradeoff for compute-intensive tasks, as evidenced by poor performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. It is suitable for lightweight database caching, text processing, and basic single-threaded applications requiring a large memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of memory, running on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz. This x86_64 architecture server features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core stress-ng CPU result, contrasted by poor multi-core and database performance. Memory bandwidth is generally weak to poor, though Passmark memory scores remain average. Qualitatively, this server provides high memory capacity relative to its compute footprint, making it suitable for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as caching, text processing, and lightweight single-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v5 is an EASv5-family VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It offers high memory density but exhibits weak multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v5 is an EASv5 VM with 2 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine offering high memory density per vCPU for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads without local storage.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 32.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance contrasted by weak multi-core capabilities
